info.name: Referral — Pharmacy Dispensing (Edge)
info.description:
### Quick-start: environment setup

1. Import **Referral_Dev_Environment.json** into Postman (Environments -> Import).
2. Select the **Referral Dev** environment from the environment picker.
3. Set `bpp_uri`, `bap_uri`, and `auth_token` for your sandbox. `bpp_uri` points at the
   Care Coordinator for the T1 folder and at the Referred Provider for the T2 folder —
   switch it between the two transactions.
4. `network_id` is currently `example.net/health` (placeholder) — confirm with the registry team.
5. UUID variable defaults are pre-filled; `coordination_id` is the load-bearing link
   (T1 issues it, T2 carries it as `coordinationRef`). Keep it identical across both folders.

## Referral — Pharmacy Dispensing (Edge case)

**Scenario:** A prescriber (Dr. Latha Rao) refers patient Karthik Nair to a specialty
pharmacy to dispense a scheme-covered specialty drug. The **prescription is the handover
Document**.

**Two-transaction model**
- **T1 — Referral Coordination:** BAP = Prescriber NP, BPP = Pharmacy Coordination Desk.
  HealthReferral. The prescription Document is referenced by `referralNote.artifactRef`
  (`{{prescription_doc_ref}}`) and lives in `contract.descriptor.docs[]`.
- **T2 — Appointment / Booking:** BAP = the SAME CC, BPP = Pharmacy. HealthContract,
  `coordinationRef = {{coordination_id}}`.

**Targeting mode:** Named pharmacy (recommendedTargetRef present); T2 skips discover.

**Consent class:** B.

**Scheme pre-auth:** T1 lifecycleState reaches **SCHEME_PREAUTH_PENDING**, which (per
HealthReferral) requires `schemeRef`. Outcome = a **dispensing confirmation**
(FHIR/MedicationDispense), not a clinical outcome note.

**Deviation shown:** ELIGIBILITY_EXPIRED — scheme pre-auth / eligibility fails;
CC cancels T2 and updates T1 (lifecycleState=CANCELLED, deviation.reasonCode ELIGIBILITY_EXPIRED).

---

### ⚠ Schema caveat — `healthServiceType: PHARMACY_DISPENSING` is non-standard

`HealthContract.healthServiceType` (and HealthOffer/HealthResource/HealthPerformance)
currently enumerate only:
`DIAGNOSTIC_ANALYTICS`, `TELECONSULTATION`, `PHYSICAL_CONSULTATION`, `RADIOLOGY`, `LAB_TEST`.

There is **no pharmacy value**. This collection uses `PHARMACY_DISPENSING` to model the
flow faithfully, but it **requires a backward-compatible enum addition** to
`healthServiceType` across HealthContract, HealthOffer, HealthResource and
HealthPerformance before it will validate against the published v2.1 health pack.
Until then, treat this collection as a proposal/illustration, not a wire-valid payload.

**T1 -> T2 link:** `coordination_id` -> T2 `coordinationRef`.


### healthServiceType status (updated)
`PHARMACY_DISPENSING` has now been **added as a backward-compatible value** to the `healthServiceType` enum across `HealthContract`, `HealthOffer`, `HealthResource`, `HealthPerformance`, and `HealthEntitlement` (and the HealthContract vocab). This flow is therefore wire-valid. Pharmacy remains a *fulfilment* edge of the referral model: the outcome is a dispensing confirmation (`FHIR/MedicationDispense`), not a clinical outcome note.

item.name: T1 · Referral Coordination  (BAP Referrer → BPP Care Coordinator)
item.description: Edge referral: prescriber -> pharmacy for a specialty drug. The prescription is the handover Document (referralNote.artifactRef -> prescription doc). Consent Class B. T1 reaches SCHEME_PREAUTH_PENDING (scheme pre-auth) so schemeRef is required.

request.description: Referrer discovers a Care Coordinator able to handle this referral category.
Care Coordinator returns its coordination catalogue entry.
Referrer originates DRAFT coordination contract (no id; CC assigns at on_select).
CC accepts; assigns coordination contract id. (= the Referral ID at on_confirm.)
Referrer confirms binding intent; full participant detail supplied.
CC returns binding quote for the coordination fee.
Referrer confirms. CONSENT (class B) is the precondition for issuance.
★ CC issues the Referral. coordinationId = {{coordination_id}} is the persistent Referral ID, consumed by T2 as coordinationRef.
Referrer polls coordination state.
CC reports lifecycleState BOOKING_CONFIRMED; targetBookingRef = {{appointment_id}}.

item.name: T2 · Appointment / Booking  (BAP Care Coordinator → BPP Referred Provider)
item.description: CC books the named pharmacy. healthServiceType=PHARMACY_DISPENSING (non-standard enum — see collection description caveat). Outcome = a dispensing confirmation, not a clinical outcome note.

request.description: CC books the NAMED provider directly via select (discover skipped). coordinationRef = {{coordination_id}} links this booking to T1.
Provider accepts; assigns appointment contract id.
CC confirms binding intent; referral note handed over via descriptor.docs[] + inputArtifacts.
Provider returns binding quote and confirmed appointment slot.
CC confirms the appointment.
★ Provider confirms appointment {{appointment_id}}. CC then sets T1 lifecycleState BOOKING_CONFIRMED.
CC polls the appointment / awaits outcome.
★ Provider reports ATTENDED and delivers the dispensing confirmation (outcomeNoteRef). CC projects outcome onto T1 (OUTCOME_RECEIVED).

item.name: Deviation — ELIGIBILITY_EXPIRED
item.description: **Deviation: ELIGIBILITY_EXPIRED.** Scheme pre-auth/eligibility fails. CC cancels T2 and updates T1 (lifecycleState=CANCELLED, deviation.reasonCode=ELIGIBILITY_EXPIRED).

request.description: CC cancels the T2 appointment (Eligibility Expired).
Provider acknowledges the T2 cancellation.

item.name: 03 — T1 update — lifecycleState CANCELLED + deviation (access revoked)

request.description: CC updates T1: lifecycleState=CANCELLED, deviation=ELIGIBILITY_EXPIRED; handover access revoked.
Referrer acknowledges terminal referral state.

variable.description: Confirm exact value with the network registry team.
Target BPP base URL for the active transaction (CC for T1, Provider for T2).
BAP callback base URL for on_* simulations.
Care Coordinator NP (BPP in T1, BAP in T2).
T1 coordination contract id = Referral ID. Consumed by T2 as coordinationRef.
Pharmacy: prescription is the handover Document.
